codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of all 4 new set bonus special effects (critDoubleStrike, doubleShot, freeCast, dodgeCounter) with CombatSystem helper methods supporting mock RNG for deterministic testing. The forceCrit parameter addition to calculateDamage is clean and correctly wired. ZoneScene integration follows existing patterns. Pre-existing deathSave, killHealPercent, and thornsHeal implementations were verified and are functionally correct. 44 well-structured unit tests with edge cases and boundary conditions. Minor inconsistencies noted below are non-blocking.

issues.description: thornsHeal directly calculates `Math.floor(this.player.maxHp * eq.thornsHeal / 100)` instead of calling the newly added `this.combatSystem.calcThornsHeal()` helper. The logic is equivalent but creates inconsistency — new procs use CombatSystem helpers while pre-existing ones don't. Same issue applies to killHealPercent (line 1473) and deathSave (line 1175) not using their respective helpers.

thornsHeal heals the player (line 1148) but does not emit PLAYER_HEALTH_CHANGED event, unlike killHealPercent (line 1474) which does. This means the HP bar UI may not update immediately after thorns healing. Pre-existing issue, not introduced by this feature.

The deathSave test 'verifies deathSave is in ZoneScene' only asserts `Math.floor(1000 * 0.3) === 300` — a trivial math check. It doesn't actually test the ZoneScene flow (lethal damage prevention, 60s cooldown, hp restoration). Given Phaser scene mocking complexity this is understandable, but the test name implies more coverage than exists.

killHealPercent test 'verifies killHealPercent stat is present in set definitions' simply asserts `expect(true).toBe(true)` with a comment listing set definitions. This is a placeholder test that provides no actual verification.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker created CombatSystem helper methods (calcThornsHeal, calcKillHeal, checkDeathSave) that mirror logic already inline in ZoneScene, but did not refactor the existing ZoneScene code to use them. This creates a dual-path inconsistency: new procs go through CombatSystem helpers while pre-existing procs use inline calculations. AGENTS.md doesn't document a convention about whether combat proc logic should live in CombatSystem helpers vs inline in ZoneScene.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: ZoneScene.ts:1147 uses inline `Math.floor(this.player.maxHp * eq.thornsHeal / 100)` while new critDoubleStrike (line 1239) uses `this.combatSystem.checkCritDoubleStrike()`. The helpers at CombatSystem.ts lines 588-623 are tested but not called from ZoneScene for the pre-existing procs.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No convention documented about whether healing effects should emit PLAYER_HEALTH_CHANGED. thornsHeal heals without emitting, killHealPercent emits. The inconsistency could cause UI desync.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: ZoneScene.ts:1146-1148 (thornsHeal, no event) vs ZoneScene.ts:1472-1475 (killHealPercent, emits PLAYER_HEALTH_CHANGED)

summary: Feature passes review. All 4 new set bonus effects (critDoubleStrike, doubleShot, freeCast, dodgeCounter) are correctly implemented with CombatSystem helper methods, mock RNG support, and ZoneScene integration. Pre-existing deathSave, killHealPercent, and thornsHeal implementations verified correct. 44 unit tests cover all 7 procs with edge cases. Non-blocking issues: (1) pre-existing procs don't use the newly created CombatSystem helpers, (2) thornsHeal doesn't emit PLAYER_HEALTH_CHANGED event unlike killHealPercent, (3) two tests are effectively placeholder assertions. Overall, solid implementation following project conventions.
